opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help — my daughter ate something with nuts and now her throat feels like it's swelling shut, her skin is all red and covered in hives, and she can't catch her breath. Her EpiPen didn't work!
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on within minutes of eating a baked good and it's getting worse fast — the autoinjector on the floor misfired.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 15 minutes ago, a few minutes after she ate a baked good at a friend's place
symptom_narrative.character: started with intense itching and hives all over, then her face and eyes puffed up, now her throat feels like it's closing and her breathing is noisy
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: she says it's the worst she's ever felt — like something terrible is about to happen
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and worsening since it started
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps getting worse on its own

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: adolescent, lives with parents, parent is present and made the call

objective_data_offered.value: SpO2 92% on a home fingertip oximeter
visible swelling of the lips, eyelids and tongue; widespread raised red hives over the trunk and limbs; audible harsh noisy breathing
parent reports she looks flushed and sweaty, is sitting leaning forward on the floor, very distressed and struggling to breathe
half-eaten baked good on the counter; adrenaline auto-injector on the floor, misfired
